steps.title: Compare both foods
steps.content: The goal is not just to mention vitamin C; it is to compare the vitamin C content of brussels sprouts and apples. The notes show both contain ascorbic acid, but brussels sprouts contain 80 mg per 100 g while apples contain 6 mg per 100 g.

steps.title: Remove one-sided options
steps.content: Choice A discusses only brussels sprouts. Choice C compares food categories rather than vitamin C amounts. Choice D explains how humans get ascorbic acid but does not compare the two foods. The needed sentence states that both foods contain vitamin C and that brussels sprouts contain more. The answer is B.
